title: 2000 Super League Grand Final
wikitext:
{{Short description|none}} {{Infobox rugby league football match |year = 2000 |title = Super League Grand Final |image = |imagesize = |alt = 2000 Super League logo |home = [[Wigan Warriors]] |away = [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] |home_abbr = WIG {{leagueicon|Wigan Warriors|16}} |away_abbr = ST H {{leagueicon|St Helens|16}} |home_half1 = 4 |home_half2 = 12 |home_total = 16 |away_half1 = 11 |away_half2 = 18 |away_total = 29 |date = 14 October 2000 |stadium = [[Old Trafford (football ground)|Old Trafford]] |location = {{flagicon|ENG}} Manchester |MoM_title = [[Harry Sunderland Trophy]] |MoM = {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Chris Joynt]] ({{leagueicon|St Helens|16}} [[St Helens RFC|St Helens]]) |anthem_title = [[And did those feet in ancient time|Jerusalem]] |anthem = |referee = [[Russell Smith (referee)|Russell Smith]] |attendance = 58,132{{cite book|last1=Butler |first1=Cliff |last2=Ponting |first2=Ivan |title=Manchester United Official Yearbook 2001: The Definitive Guide to the 2000−2001 Season |page=215 |publisher=Carlton Books Limited |date=June 2000 |isbn=978-0-2339995-2-4}} |network = [[Sky Sports]] |commentators = [[Eddie Hemmings (rugby league)|Eddie Hemmings]] |commentators2= [[Mike Stephenson]] |commentators3= |tournaments = [[Super League Grand Final]] |last = [[1999 Super League Grand Final|1999]] |next = [[2001 Super League Grand Final|2001]] }} The '''2000 Super League Grand Final''' was the '''third official Grand Final''' and the conclusive and championship-deciding game of 2000's [[Super League V]]. Held on Saturday 14 October 2000 at [[Old Trafford (football ground)|Old Trafford]], [[Manchester]], the game was played between [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] and [[Wigan Warriors]]. Wigan wore blue for the encounter and St Helens wore their traditional red and white. The match was refereed by Russell Smith of Castleford and played before a crowd of 58,132. In the end St Helens, inspired by their captain [[Chris Joynt]], defeated Wigan Warriors 29–16.{{Cite news | last = news.bbc.co.uk | title = Joynt inspires Saints triumph | work = BBC Sport | place = UK | publisher = BBC | date = 14 October 2000 | url = https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/972485.stm | access-date = 28 November 2010}} ==Background== {{further|Super League V}} '''[[Super League V|Tetleys Super League V]]''' reverted to 12 teams after [[Gateshead Thunder (1999)|Gateshead Thunder]] and [[Sheffield Eagles]] left the league although [[Huddersfield Giants]] subsequently became '''Huddersfield-Sheffield Giants''' and finished bottom for the third consecutive season. [[Wigan Warriors]] finished top for the first time since [[Super League III]] when they won the Grand Final. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |- ! style="width:20px;" abbr="Position"| !width=175 |Team !
